name: Vaxart, Inc.
business: Vaxart is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing oral recombinant vaccines using its proprietary VAAST platform. It targets infectious diseases including norovirus, coronavirus, and influenza, and is developing a therapeutic vaccine for HPV. The company also earns royalties from anti-influenza drugs Relenza and Inavir.
sellsTo: High-risk groups (infants, children up to five years, older adults, elderly)
Healthcare, childcare, and elder care workers
General population (influenza vaccination recommended for individuals six months and older)

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biotechnology company primarily focused on the development of oral recombinant vaccines based on our Vector-Adjuvant-Antigen Standardized Technology (VAAST) proprietary oral vaccine platform.

We are developing prophylactic vaccine candidates that target a range of infectious diseases, including norovirus, coronavirus, including SARS-CoV-2 (the virus that causes coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19)), and influenza.
we have generated preclinical data for our first therapeutic vaccine candidate targeting cervical cancer and dysplasia caused by human papillomavirus (HPV).
Through the 2018 Merger with Aviragen, we acquired two royalty earning products, Relenza and Inavir.

catalysts.event: 12-month follow-up data from sentinel cohort of Phase 2b COVID-19 study
catalysts.sentence: Additional data regarding the 12-month follow-up for these 400 individuals is anticipated to be shared in the [first half of 2026].

catalysts.event: Modification No. 6 to 2024 ATI-RRPV Contract increasing funding to $316.0 million
catalysts.sentence: On March 10, 2026, we entered into Modification No. 6 to the 2024 ATI-RRPV Contract, pursuant to which the total amount of funding available for payment under the 2024 ATI-RRPV Contract was increased to $316.0 million.

catalysts.event: Altesa announces $75 million Series B funding for Vapendavir Phase 2b study
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, Altesa announced a $75 million Series B funding round to support the Phase 2b study.

tamClaims.claim: Total global annual economic burden of norovirus estimated at $60 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: In a study published by Johns Hopkins University and the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) in 2016, the total global annual economic burden of norovirus was estimated at $60 billion.

tamClaims.claim: Annual economic impact of norovirus to the U.S. estimated at $10.6 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: In a more recent health economic study published in the Journal of Infectious Diseases in July 2020, the economic impact to the U.S. was estimated to be $10.6 billion annually.

tamClaims.claim: Around one billion cases of seasonal influenza annually worldwide, with 3-5 million severe cases and 290,000-650,000 deaths.
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that there are around one billion cases of seasonal influenza annually worldwide, of which 3 million to 5 million cases are considered severe, causing 290,000 to 650,000 deaths per year globally.

tamClaims.claim: In the U.S., 9-41 million influenza cases, 140,000-710,000 hospitalizations, and 12,000-52,000 deaths annually.
tamClaims.sentence: In the U.S., between 9,000,000 and 41,000,000 people catch influenza annually, between 140,000 and 710,000 people are hospitalized with complications of influenza, and between 12,000 and 52,000 people die from influenza and its complications each year.

tamClaims.claim: Cervical cancer is the fourth most common cancer in women worldwide; about 13,000 new cases diagnosed annually in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: Cervical cancer is the fourth most common cancer in women worldwide, and about 13,000 new cases are diagnosed annually in the U.S. according to the National Cervical Cancer Coalition.

tamClaims.claim: 80 million U.S. citizens currently infected with HPV (25% of population), with 14 million new infections per year.
tamClaims.sentence: The CDC estimates 80 million U.S. citizens are currently infected with HPV, representing 25% of the population, with about 14 million new infections per year.

tamClaims.claim: More than 400,000 women diagnosed with CIN annually in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: More than 400,000 women are diagnosed with CIN annually in the U.S., with an annual incidence estimate for CIN1 and CIN2/3 at 1.6 and 1.2 per 1,000 women, respectively.
